# SEO audit — site-wide metadata, canonical, social, structured data

**Generated:** 2026-04-25
**Scope:** All 646 HTML files under inventory; **483 public-indexable** pages after excluding `admin/`, `config/`, `noindex`, and exempt pages (`404.html`, `offline.html`).
**Source data:** [`reports/seo/inventory.json`](./seo/inventory.json) (committed artefact regenerated by [`scripts/node/inventory-seo.js`](../scripts/node/inventory-seo.js)) + secondary scan via [`scripts/node/seo-audit.js`](../scripts/node/seo-audit.js).
**Method:** read-only aggregation of the inventory artefact. **No live Lighthouse / CrUX / Linkinator runs in this audit** — those are gated by network access and called out below as deferred for the fix PR.

This audit is the **Wave A deliverable for Track 2 (SEO)** of the multi-track quality program — see [`docs/plans/2026-04-25_multi-track-quality.md`](../docs/plans/2026-04-25_multi-track-quality.md). It informs three Wave B fix PRs (2.1 metadata, 2.2 structured data, 2.4 social previews + canonical helper). Internal-linking (§2.3) is a separate audit deliverable (`reports/internal-linking.md`) that needs a built `dist/` link graph; **deferred** from this PR.

Per `AGENTS.md` §6.4 and §6.11: this audit records what is on the site today; it does not move canonicals, sitemap, `og:*`, or `CNAME`. It separates measured findings from items that require a separate live run.

---

## 1. Headline numbers

| Surface | Coverage on public-indexable pages | Notes |
| --- | ---: | --- |
| `<title>` present | 483 / 483 (100%) | — |
| `<meta name="description">` present | 483 / 483 (100%) | — |
| `<link rel="canonical">` present | 483 / 483 (100%) | All on `https://goldtickerlive.com/` apex (no `www.`, no `http:`). CNAME parity holds. |
| `og:title` / `og:description` / `og:image` / `og:url` / `og:type` | 483 / 483 each (100%) | — |
| `twitter:card` | 483 / 483 (100%) | — |
| `twitter:image` | 483 / 483 (100%) | — |
| `<link rel="alternate" hreflang>` (≥ 2 entries) | 483 / 483 (100%) | EN + AR pairs from `inventory-seo.js` summary: 634 across all records (including admin / non-indexable). |
| JSON-LD present (≥ 1 graph) | 483 / 483 (100%) | Validity not re-checked here; `inventory-seo.js` reports 0 parse failures across all 646 records. |

**Read:** the existing `check-seo-meta.js` validate-gate is doing its job — no presence gaps. The audit findings below are about **values** (length, uniqueness, schema-vs-DOM correctness), not missing tags.

---

## 2. Track 2.1 — Metadata findings (titles, descriptions, duplicates)

### 2.1.A Title length

**394 / 483 (81.6%) public-indexable titles exceed 60 characters.**

Google typically truncates SERP titles around 50–60 chars (≈ 580 px). Truncation directly costs CTR.

Distribution by template family:

| Template | Long-title pages | Worst length |
| --- | ---: | ---: |
| `countries/<c>/<city>/gold-rate/<karat>/index.html` | 276 | up to 75 chars |
| `countries/<c>/<city>/gold-prices/index.html` | 69 | **80 chars** (e.g. `Gold Price in Islamabad, Pakistan Today — 24K, 22K, 21K, 18K in PKR \| GoldPrices`) |
| `countries/<c>/<city>/gold-shops/index.html` | 15 | — |
| `countries/<c>/gold-price/index.html` | 13 | — |
| `countries/<other>` (city `index.html`, etc.) | 8 | — |
| `countries/<c>/markets/<slug>.html` | 2 | — |
| `content/guides/*` | 4 | 77 chars (`The AED–USD Fixed Peg Explained — Why UAE Gold Prices Are Stable \| GoldPrices`) |
| `content/<other>` | 4 | — |
| Other | 3 | — |

**Recommendation (fix PR — Wave B 2.1):** rework the city-template title generator to drop the `, <Country>` clause when the city slug is unambiguous, drop the trailing ` | GoldPrices` suffix on country-aware pages (already implied by canonical), and shorten the karat list to `24K/22K/21K/18K`. Target: every public title ≤ 60 chars.

Two short-title outliers are intentional (legal pages, fine):

| Path | Length | Title |
| --- | ---: | --- |
| `privacy.html` | 27 | `Privacy Policy \| GoldPrices` |
| `terms.html` | 29 | `Terms of Service \| GoldPrices` |

### 2.1.B Meta-description length

**13 / 483 descriptions exceed 160 characters** (Google truncates around 155–160 for desktop SERPs). All under 200.

Top offenders:

| Path | Length |
| --- | ---: |
| `content/guides/aed-peg-explained.html` | 198 |
| `shops.html` | 198 |
| `content/guides/invest-in-gold-gcc.html` | 188 |
| `countries/uae/markets/dubai-gold-souk.html` | 188 |
| `content/guides/buying-guide.html` | 185 |

No descriptions are **too short** (< 70 chars).
No descriptions are **missing**.

**Recommendation (fix PR — Wave B 2.1):** trim the 13 long descriptions to ≤ 158 chars. EN+AR parity required if the page has an Arabic counterpart (see translations file). No DOM change.

### 2.1.C Duplicate titles / descriptions

- **Duplicate title groups:** 1 (2 pages) — both literally `Gold Shops in Tripoli — Dealers & Jewellers | GoldPrices`:
  - `countries/lebanon/tripoli/gold-shops/index.html`
  - `countries/libya/tripoli/gold-shops/index.html`
  Same city name in two different countries; should disambiguate (`Tripoli, Lebanon` / `Tripoli, Libya`).
- **Duplicate meta-description groups:** 0.

**Recommendation (fix PR — Wave B 2.1):** disambiguate the two Tripoli shop-listing pages.

### 2.1.D Canonical

- 0 missing canonicals on indexable pages.
- 0 `www.` canonicals.
- 0 `http:` canonicals.
- All canonicals start with `https://goldtickerlive.com/` (matches `CNAME`).

No findings. Track 2.4 canonical pillar is healthy and must stay healthy (§6.4 trust guardrail).

---

## 3. Track 2.2 — Structured-data findings

### 3.1 Per-type coverage on public-indexable pages

| `@type` | Pages |
| --- | ---: |
| `BreadcrumbList` | 482 |
| `Product` | 91 |
| `Article` | 7 |
| `Organization` | 1 |
| `WebSite` | 1 |
| `FAQPage` | **0** |
| `LocalBusiness` | **0** |
| `WebApplication` | **0** |

`Organization` + `WebSite` (with `SearchAction`) live on `index.html` — confirmed via grep `potentialAction|SearchAction` (2 hits). Track H §11 requirement met for the homepage.

### 3.2 Visible-content gaps to fix in the JSON-LD pass

The plan's §6.1 / §6.7 / §6.8 guardrail rule applies: **never add a JSON-LD field whose data isn't visible on the page.** All recommendations below are gated on DOM presence; the validator script proposed in §2.2 of the plan must enforce that.

| Gap | Page(s) | Recommendation |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Visible FAQ but no `FAQPage` schema | `content/faq/index.html` | Add `FAQPage` listing the actual `<dt>/<dd>` (or accordion) pairs rendered. Bilingual mainEntity if AR copy is rendered. |
| Tracker page has `BreadcrumbList` only — no `WebApplication` | `tracker.html` (and any `pages/tracker.html` shim) | Track H §11 explicitly calls for `WebApplication` here. Add it after confirming visible interactive UI. |
| Methodology has no `Article` | `methodology.html` | Article schema mirrors visible H1 + author/published metadata, where present. |
| News index has no `Article`/`CollectionPage` | `content/news/index.html` | Add only if/when individual news items are rendered with dates + author. Currently a hub — `CollectionPage` is the right choice if items are listed, else `WebPage` is fine. |
| 71 city-shop hub pages (`countries/<c>/<city>/gold-shops/`) carry only `BreadcrumbList` | (full list in inventory) | If individual shop entries with `name`, `address`, `geo`, `url` are rendered, emit an `ItemList` of `LocalBusiness` items. Skip pages that just say "no shops yet" — schema would lie. |
| `content/guides/index.html` (guides hub) has no `Article` | hub page | Hub → `CollectionPage` with `ItemList` of guides actually linked. |
| `content/order-gold/`, `content/premium-watch/`, `content/tools/`, `content/social/`, `content/changelog/`, `content/embed/`, `content/compare-countries/`, `content/todays-best-rates/`, `content/submit-shop/` | listed | Audit each for the right canonical type; do **not** stuff `Article` onto interactive tools. |

### 3.3 Schema-vs-content trust-guardrail risks (don't slip up here)

- `Product` / `Offer` is currently used on 91 country/city `gold-prices/` pages. Inventory data shows these pages render reference prices, not retail offers. **Per `AGENTS.md` §6.1, `Product` schema must label these as reference prices** — verify each `offers.priceCurrency` and `description` says "reference / spot-derived" not "retail." Action item for the Wave B 2.2 PR: read the rendered DOM, ensure schema mirrors the visible `freshness` pill state, and **drop the `Product` schema on any page whose visible content has switched to "no live data."**
- `BreadcrumbList` `itemListElement[*].item` URLs must be absolute apex URLs (`https://goldtickerlive.com/...`) and match the page's canonical chain. Spot-check 3 country/city pages in the fix PR.

### 3.4 Validator (additive, no behavior change)

The fix PR (Wave B 2.2) should add `scripts/node/check-jsonld-dom.js` that:

1. Parses each page's JSON-LD graphs (already proven to be 0 parse failures by `inventory-seo.js`).
2. For each `@type`, asserts the corresponding DOM element / text exists. Examples:
   - `FAQPage.mainEntity[*].name` → must appear as a heading or summary in the DOM.
   - `Article.headline` → must appear as the visible H1.
   - `Product.offers.price` → only allowed if a visible price element with the same numeric value renders **and** carries a freshness label.
3. Fails CI on mismatch. This is the validator the plan §2.2 calls for.

---

## 4. Track 2.4 — Social previews & social-graph findings

| Surface | Status | Notes |
| --- | --- | --- |
| `og:image` present | 483 / 483 | — |
| `og:image` dimensions correct (1200×630) | **not measured here** | Audit reads tag values, not image binaries. The fix PR should run an additive script that fetches each unique `og:image` URL, reads dimensions, asserts ≥ 1200×630 and aspect ratio 1.91:1 (Twitter recommends ≥ 600×314, FB recommends 1200×630). |
| `og:url` matches canonical | **not cross-checked here** | Two pages of risk: any page whose `og:url` was hand-written before the canonical helper landed. Recommend the fix PR adds a derived assertion in `check-seo-meta.js`: `og:url === canonical`. |
| `og:locale` + `og:locale:alternate` (EN ↔ AR pair) | **not measured here** | The inventory captures `hreflang` pairs (100% have ≥ 2), but `og:locale[:alternate]` is not currently captured by `inventory-seo.js`. The fix PR should extend the inventory schema to record both, then assert mutuality. |
| `<link rel="alternate" hreflang>` mutual pairs | 100% have ≥ 2 entries; full mutuality check defers to `tests/seo-sitewide.test.js` | No new finding here; existing test owns it. |

**Recommendation (fix PR — Wave B 2.4):**

1. Extend `inventory-seo.js` to record `og:locale`, `og:locale:alternate`, and the parsed `og:image` URL → asset filesystem path.
2. Add a `check-og-image-dimensions.js` step (additive, runs only on changed pages in CI to keep it fast).
3. Add `og:url === canonical` cross-check to `check-seo-meta.js`.
4. Where missing, consolidate per-page metadata into a single `setHead({ title, desc, canonical, ogImage, locale })` helper used by template generators — no churn-only refactor; only touch templates already being modified by the fix PR.

---

## 5. Items deferred to a separate audit (need network / build)

These are flagged here **so the fix PR doesn't accidentally claim coverage:**

| Item | Why deferred | Where it belongs |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Lighthouse SEO + Core Web Vitals (LCP / CLS / INP) on top 20 templates | Needs Chromium + a serving build; sandbox can't run it deterministically. | A separate `reports/cwv-baseline.md` produced via `npx lighthouse` on a built `dist/` (Wave B 2.1 fix PR can attach the run if CI is wired). |
| Linkinator broken-link sweep on built `dist/` | Needs a full `npm run build` + Linkinator with allow-list of expected externals. | Folded into the broken-link slice of Wave B 2.1 (`reports/broken-links.md`). |
| Site-wide internal-link graph (orphans, weak hubs) | Needs `dist/` HTML to walk. Same blocker as above. | `reports/internal-linking.md` (plan §2.3). |
| Image dimension validation for `og:image` assets | Needs to read PNG/JPEG headers from `assets/` for every distinct URL. | Wave B 2.4 fix PR; trivial to ship. |
| Google Rich Results Test parity | Needs Google API access. | Optional — local schema validation via `schemarama`/`sdo-validator` is sufficient. |

These are the only audit pillars from the plan that this PR does not claim. Everything in §2 and §3 above is grounded in committed inventory data.

---

## 6. Suggested Wave B fix PRs (output of this audit)

The fix work splits naturally into three small PRs:

| Fix PR | Scope | Approx pages touched |
| --- | --- | --- |
| **B-1: title + description trim** | City `gold-rate/<karat>/`, city `gold-prices/`, country `gold-price/`, content guides; trim 13 long descriptions; disambiguate Tripoli. | ≈ 396 templates / template generators (most via 1–2 generator changes) |
| **B-2: structured data — visible-content alignment** | Add `FAQPage` to `content/faq/`; add `WebApplication` to `tracker.html`; audit + correct the 91 `Product` pages for trust-label alignment; add `ItemList`/`LocalBusiness` only where shop names are actually rendered. Ship `check-jsonld-dom.js`. | ≈ 100 pages + 1 new validator |
| **B-3: social previews + canonical-helper consolidation** | Extend `inventory-seo.js` to record `og:locale[:alternate]`; add `og:url === canonical` cross-check; add `og:image` dimension check; consolidate `setHead()` only in templates already being modified above. | All public pages (additive checks) + ≤ 5 templates |

Each fix PR follows the cross-cutting guardrails in [`docs/plans/2026-04-25_multi-track-quality.md`](../docs/plans/2026-04-25_multi-track-quality.md): bilingual EN+AR copy parity, no canonical movement, no `www.` reintroduction, no schema for invisible content, freshness labels preserved.

---

## 7. Verification of this audit itself

- Inventory artefact is the committed `reports/seo/inventory.json` — value drift would have failed `npm run validate` (`inventory-seo.js --check`), so the input is trustworthy.
- All counts in §1, §2, §3.1 reproducible by re-running `node scripts/node/inventory-seo.js --stdout` and aggregating per the same predicates (excluding `admin/`, `config/`, `noindex`, `exempt`).
- No code changes shipped. No SEO surface modified. Canonical apex (`goldtickerlive.com`) untouched.
